nodes:
module:autofix_commit_msg=|
    chore: auto fixes from pre-commit.ci hooks

    for more information, see https://pre-commit.ci
module:name=validate JSON schemas against meta schemas
module:description=The time of the event in GMT, EST/EDT and PDT/PDT

module:value=|
        * All are welcome to join and we record and each session for later reference.
        * Participants must abide by our [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/meltano/meltano/?tab=coc-ov-file).
        * We look forward to seeing you there, and please consider these acts of etiquette:
          * Mute your microphone when not speaking.
          * Blur your background to reduce visual distractions.
module:description=A list of topics to discuss

module:value=|
        Thanks for taking the time to fill out this bug report!
module:description=Version of Meltano you are using
module:description=Version of Python you are using
module:description=What operating system you are using
module:description=Describe what you were trying to get done
module:placeholder=Tell us what happened, what went wrong, and what you expected to happen
module:description=Paste the contents of your `meltano.yml` file, omitting any sensitive information

module:placeholder=|
        venv:
          backend: ...
        plugins:
          extractors:
          - name: tap-my-extractor
            variant: my-variant
            pip_url: ...
module:description=Provide a link to the Slack or Linen conversation, if applicable

module:value=|
        Thanks for taking the time to request a new feature!
module:description=Functionality this new feature would impact
module:description=Describe the feature you would like to see

module:run=|
        version=$(uvx --from toml-cli toml get --toml-path=pyproject.toml project.version)
        tag=$(echo "${GITHUB_REF}" | awk '{split($0,p,"/"); print p[3]}')
        if [ "v$version" != $tag ]; then echo "Release tag ('$tag') and package version ('v$version') do not match!"; exit 1; fi;

module:run=|
        echo "If this is not a dry run, the image will be published with the following tags:"
        cat tags

        echo 'IMAGE_TAGS<<EOF' >> $GITHUB_ENV
        echo "$(cat tags)" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        echo 'EOF' >> $GITHUB_ENV

module:run=|
        if [[ "${STEPS_SET_INPUTS_OUTPUTS_REGISTRY}" == "ghcr.io" ]]; then
          echo "username=${GITHUB_ACTOR}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "password=${GITHUB_TOKEN}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        fi
        if [[ "${STEPS_SET_INPUTS_OUTPUTS_REGISTRY}" == "docker.io" ]]; then
          echo "username=meltano" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "password=${DOCKERHUB_TOKEN}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        fi
module:name=Build, scan, then conditionally push the Docker image for a given Python version

module:push=${{ ( github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' ) || ( github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' ) || ( github.event_name == 'release' && github.event.action == 'published' )}}

module:run=>
        docker run -d
        -p "5432:5432"
        -e "POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres"
        -e "POSTGRES_DB=warehouse"
        --name postgres
        --health-cmd "pg_isready -d postgres -U postgres"
        --health-interval 10s
        --health-timeout 5s
        --health-retries 5
        postgres:13
module:name=GitHub Actions Security Analysis with zizmor 🌈
module:name=Install the latest version of uv
module:name=Combine coverage data and display human readable report

module:run=|
        nox -rs coverage -- combine --debug=pathmap
        nox -rs coverage -- report --show-missing --ignore-errors

module:body=|
          Bump changelog for release v${{ steps.cz-bump.outputs.version }}
module:description='Build a Docker image, scan it, then push it to a container registry'
module:description='A GitHub API token that can access the code scanning endpoints.'
module:description='The Docker tags for the image that will be built'
module:description='Whether to push the built image to the registry'
module:description='The username to use when pushing the image'
module:description='The password to use when pushing the image'
module:description='The version of Python that will be installed on the image'
module:description='Whether to build the slim variant of the image'

module:run=|
        if [[ "${INPUTS_SLIM}" == "true" ]]; then
          echo "path=docker/meltano/Dockerfile.slim" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        else
          echo "path=docker/meltano/Dockerfile" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        fi
module:name=Build the image for all supported architectures
module:name=Load the amd64 image for scanning
module:name=Get the ID of the image

module:run=|
        docker images
        echo "id=$(docker images -q --no-trunc ${INPUTS_REGISTRY}/meltano/meltano | head -n 1)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

module:run=|
        echo "View scan results at:"
        echo "https://github.com/meltano/meltano/security/code-scanning?query=ref:${GITHUB_REF}+tool:Grype"
        sleep 10 # Give GitHub some time to process the uploaded report
        NUM_ISSUES="$(curl --no-progress-meter -H "Authorization: token ${INPUTS_TOKEN}" \
            "https://api.github.com/repos/meltano/meltano/code-scanning/alerts?tool_name=Grype&state=open&ref=${GITHUB_REF}" \
            | jq length)"
        [ "$NUM_ISSUES" = '0' ] # Error if there are any alerts that are neither fixed nor dismissed
module:name=Push the scanned image to the registry
module:description='Path to the schemas for igluctl to lint'
module:import React, { useState, useEffect, useRef, useMemo } from 'react';
module:import React, {useCallback, useMemo, useRef, useState} from 'react';
module:import React, {useCallback, useState, useRef, useEffect} from 'react';

module:__filter__=>
            ('tap-' in name or 'target-' in full_name)
            and name != 'singer-tap-template'
            and name != 'singer-target-template'

module:description=Clones all production schemas in the PREP database that the user
          has access to.
module:query=tap- fork:false language:Python singer in:readme org:MeltanoLabs org:transferwise

module:description=Whether to share anonymous usage data with the Meltano team using Snowplow. This helps determine high impact changes for future releases and provides usage data on MeltanoHub.
module:description=Used to uniquely identify your project if send_anonymous_usage_stats is enabled.
module:description=URI for the system database that stores Meltano metadata. Should be a valid SQLAlchemy database URL.
module:description=Maximum number of reconnection attempts for initial database connection.
module:description=Retry interval in seconds for initial database connection attempts.
module:description=When enabled, blocks modifications to project files through the CLI in this environment.
module:description=Root URL for the hub API. If provided, this overrides the hub_url setting.
module:description=URL where Meltano can find the Hub that lists all discoverable plugins.
module:description=Value of the Authorization header sent when making requests to hub_url.
module:description=Size in bytes of the buffer between extractor and loader that stores Singer messages.
module:description=Python version to use for plugins, specified as a path or executable name. Can be overridden per-plugin.
module:description=Whether to automatically install required plugins on command invocation.
module:description=URI for the state backend where you'd like Meltano to store state.
module:description=Number of seconds that a lock for a state ID should be considered valid in a state backend
module:description=Number of seconds that a Meltano should wait if trying to access or modify state for a state ID that is locked
module:description=Path to a logging configuration file for the CLI
module:description=Short-hand for the log format to use
module:description=Whether to disable checking for Meltano updates on CLI commands. Can also be controlled via MELTANO_CLI_DISABLE_VERSION_CHECK environment variable.

module:description=Whether to fail if an environment variable is not defined

module:description=Whether to share anonymous usage data with the Meltano team using Snowplow. This helps determine high impact changes for future releases and provides usage data on MeltanoHub.
module:description=Used to uniquely identify your project if send_anonymous_usage_stats is enabled.
module:description=URI for the system database that stores Meltano metadata. Should be a valid SQLAlchemy database URL.
module:description=Maximum number of reconnection attempts for initial database connection.
module:description=Retry interval in seconds for initial database connection attempts.
module:description=When enabled, blocks modifications to project files through the CLI in this environment.
module:description=Root URL for the hub API. If provided, this overrides the hub_url setting.
module:description=URL where Meltano can find the Hub that lists all discoverable plugins.
module:description=Value of the Authorization header sent when making requests to hub_url.
module:description=Size in bytes of the buffer between extractor and loader that stores Singer messages.
module:description=Python version to use for plugins, specified as a path or executable name. Can be overridden per-plugin.
module:description=Whether to automatically install required plugins on command invocation.
module:description=URI for the state backend where you'd like Meltano to store state.
module:description=Number of seconds that a lock for a state ID should be considered valid in a state backend
module:description=Number of seconds that a Meltano should wait if trying to access or modify state for a state ID that is locked
module:description=Path to a logging configuration file for the CLI
module:description=Short-hand for the log format to use
module:description=Whether to disable checking for Meltano updates on CLI commands. Can also be controlled via MELTANO_CLI_DISABLE_VERSION_CHECK environment variable.
module:description=Backend to use for creating virtual environments
module:description=Whether to fail if an environment variable is not defined
